analysis.headline: Myanmar earthquake: at least 144 killed as rescues also continue in Bangkok
analysis.most_supported_version: Dry run: this cluster is reported by Independent / Unknown with weighted support 1.88.
analysis.compiled_body: Myanmar earthquake: at least 144 killed as rescues also continue in Bangkok: (25%) 144 dead | (25%) 730 injured. Myanmar earthquake: at least 144 killed as rescues also continue in Bangkok Junta makes rare call for aid as hundreds injured by 7.7-magnitude quake, which also brought down high-rise in Thai capital At least 144 people have died and 732 have been injured after a powerful earthquake struck Myanmar, killing at least nine more in neighbouring Thailand where a high-rise building under construction in Bangkok collapsed. The 7.7-magnitude earthquake hit central Myanmar at 12.50pm local time on Friday at a depth of 10km (6.2 miles). Its epicentre was about 11 miles (17.2km) from Mandalay, Myanmar’s second-largest city. Bangkok skyscraper collapses after Myanmar earthquakes A powerful earthquake has hit Myanmar and neighbouring Thailand, destroying buildings, damaging infrastructure and killing more than 150 people. The head of Myanmar’s military government, Senior General Min Aung Hlaing, said that at least 144 people were killed and 730 others were injured in his country. This dry-run recap is based on 4 article extract(s), reported by Independent / Unknown, with weighted support 1.88 and average pair similarity 0.24.

analysis.headline: Myanmar struck by 6.8-magnitude earthquake
analysis.most_supported_version: Dry run: this cluster is reported by Independent / Unknown with weighted support 0.47.
analysis.compiled_body: Myanmar struck by 6.8-magnitude earthquake. Myanmar struck by 6.8-magnitude earthquake US Geological Survey reports powerful quake in north-central Myanmar, damaging temples in the ancient city of Bagan A powerful 6.8-magnitude earthquake has hit central Myanmar , the US Geological Survey has said, severely damaging ancient temples at the old city of Bagan and killing one person. The site, which is often compared to Cambodia’s Angkor Wat, holds more than 2,500 temples and pagodas, some of them centuries old. The earthquake was so intense that it was felt in Bangkok, about 620 miles (1,000km) away. This dry-run recap is based on 1 article extract(s), reported by Independent / Unknown, with weighted support 0.47 and average pair similarity 1.00.
